inline_comments.message: I'm OK with this since we typically only run stability tests on the master branch.  However, this value could be obtained from the .gitreview file.

The CSIT file which I referred had the GERRIT_BRANCH set as master, hence followed the same.
As suggested, its good to obtain the same from .gitreview file , fixed in patch 2.

I don't think this will work as gerrit will make an assumption about which branch to pull from (probably master), which defeats the purpose.  So I guess we have a choice as to how to run the stability test:
1) if we just want to copy the relevant files (e.g., setup_pap.sh, jmx) to the target VM,
   then we can just leave the branch hard-coded, as you had it before; or
2) if we use git-clone to pull the whole pap repo onto the target VM, then we don't even need
   to determine the branch and can extract the version directly from the pom in the cloned
   repo.  Something like:
     GIT_TOP=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
     POLICY_PAP_VERSION_EXTRACT=$(xmllint --xpath
       '/*[local-name()="project"]/*[local-name()="version"]/text()'
       "${GIT_TOP}"/pom.xml)
good point on the CSIT, I'll see if that can't be fixed
